questions.q: A card is drawn from a well-shuffled deck of 52 cards. What is the probability that it is a king?
If P(A) = 0.4 and P(B) = 0.3, what is the probability that neither A nor B occurs? (Assume A and B are mutually exclusive events)
Which of the following is a requirement for two events to be mutually exclusive?
questions.answer: They cannot occur at the same time

questions.q: A bag contains 5 white balls and 7 black balls. If 2 balls are drawn at random without replacement, what is the probability that both are white?
questions.answer: Let A be the event of drawing a white ball in the first draw. P(A) = 5/12. Let B be the event of drawing a white ball in the second draw given that the first ball drawn is white. P(B|A) = 4/11. Using the multiplication rule for conditional probability, P(A ∩ B) = P(A) * P(B|A) = (5/12) * (4/11) = 20/132 = 5/33

questions.q: Given that P(A ∪ B) = 0.7, P(A) = 0.4, and P(B) = 0.3, are events A and B mutually exclusive? Justify your answer.
questions.answer: Since P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A ∩ B), we have 0.7 = 0.4 + 0.3 - P(A ∩ B). This gives P(A ∩ B) = 0.4 + 0.3 - 0.7 = 0. Thus, P(A ∩ B) = 0, which means A and B are mutually exclusive.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The probability of an impossible event is _______.
State True or False: If P(A) = 0.6 and P(B) = 0.5, then P(A ∩ B) must be greater than 0.5.
If P(A) = 0.3, P(B) = 0.4, and P(A ∪ B) = 0.5, find the probability that exactly one of them occurs.
questions.answer: P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A ∩ B). Given P(A ∪ B) = 0.5, P(A) = 0.3, and P(B) = 0.4, we can find P(A ∩ B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A ∪ B) = 0.3 + 0.4 - 0.5 = 0.2. The probability that exactly one of A or B occurs is P(A) + P(B) - 2*P(A ∩ B) = 0.3 + 0.4 - 2*0.2 = 0.3

questions.q: A fair coin is tossed. What is the probability of getting a head?
Two dice are rolled. What is the probability that the sum of the numbers on the two dice is 7?
questions.answer: There are 6 outcomes where the sum is 7: (1,6), (2,5), (3,4), (4,3), (5,2), (6,1). Total outcomes = 36. So, probability = 6/36 = 1/6
